prompt:
Subject: Charged twice for my subscription this month

Hi, I just checked my bank statement and I was charged $49.99 twice on Feb 1st for my monthly subscription. I only have one account. Please refund the duplicate charge immediately. Order refs: INV-2024-0201-A and INV-2024-0201-B.
reference_response:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We have reviewed your account and confirmed that a duplicate charge of $49.99 was applied on February 1st. We sincerely apologize for this error.

A full refund of $49.99 has been initiated to your original payment method and should appear within 3–5 business days. Reference: REF-REFUND-0201.

We are also reviewing our billing systems to prevent recurrence. Please do not hesitate to contact us if you have further concerns.

Best regards,
Customer Support Team

prompt:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing to formally lodge a complaint regarding the unacceptable delay in provisioning of the enterprise analytics module contracted under Agreement #ENT-2025-0045. The module was due on January 15, 2026. As of today, February 22, 2026, it remains unavailable. This delay has materially impacted our operations. We require an immediate written explanation and a binding delivery commitment.
reference_response:
Dear Sir or Madam,

We acknowledge receipt of your complaint regarding the delayed provisioning of the enterprise analytics module under Agreement #ENT-2025-0045. We recognize the severity of this matter and the operational impact it has caused.

Following an internal review, we can confirm that the module will be fully provisioned by March 1, 2026. A formal remediation report will be delivered to you by February 25, 2026, outlining the cause of delay and corrective measures.

We remain committed to fulfilling our contractual obligations and regret the disruption caused.

Yours sincerely,
Enterprise Support Division

prompt: Hey, I signed up for the premium plan 10 days ago and my account still says 'pending activation'. I've emailed support twice and heard nothing back. This is really frustrating — I'm paying for something I can't even use. Can someone please just fix this?
reference_response:
Hi there,

We're really sorry to hear you've been waiting 10 days without a resolution — that's not the experience we want for any of our customers, and we completely understand your frustration.

I've escalated your account activation to our provisioning team and marked it as high priority. You should receive a confirmation email within the next 2 hours, and your account will be fully active by end of day today.

As an apology for the wait, we're crediting 10 days to your subscription automatically.

Thank you for your patience, and please reach out directly if anything else comes up!

Warm regards,
Customer Experience Team

prompt: I'm honestly really upset. I've been a customer for 4 years and this month I got charged for a plan upgrade I never requested. I didn't click anything, I didn't change anything. My card was just charged an extra $30. I feel like I'm being taken advantage of.
reference_response:
Dear Valued Customer,

I'm so sorry this happened — after 4 years with us, this is absolutely not the experience you deserve, and I completely understand how upsetting an unexpected charge must feel.

I want you to know we take this very seriously. I've reviewed your account and can confirm no plan change was initiated from your side. This was an error on our end, and we're deeply sorry.

The $30 has been fully refunded and will appear on your statement within 3–5 business days. I'm also adding a $10 credit to your account as a thank-you for your loyalty and patience.

Please don't hesitate to reach out — we're here for you.

With sincere apologies,
Customer Care Team
